data.title: The Fatal Flaw in "Back to Starbucks (NASDAQ:SBUX)"
data.description:
So it has been about a year and a half now since coffee giant Starbucks (SBUX) launched on its Back to Starbucks plan, its effort to get people to show up to cafe locations, and stick around. This is Starbucks' attempt to get people out of the mindset that Starbucks is a commodity coffee place, and into the mindset that made Starbucks big: that it is a coffee house, and the center of culture that it was back in the late 90s or so.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The problem, however, is that this idea depends on one critical point that will likely turn out to be Back to Starbucks' fatal flaw: baristas. So why is the barista the weak link in the Back to Starbucks plan? What can be done instead?

The Weak Link

The baristas are the weak link in the Back to Starbucks plan, but not for the reason you might think. It is not that the baristas are faulty in some way, but rather, that the baristas largely do not have the bandwidth to handle this new task.

We know that when the program was first introduced back in September, there were concerns about staffing levels. The union at Starbucks, Starbucks Workers United, released a study that suggested serious problems, including 69% of respondents saying they saw "…uneven and mismanaged scheduling," as well as "…erratic or unpredictable scheduling…" at individual locations.

This alone would be bad enough. Not having sufficient staff to keep up with incoming customers is bad enough, but expecting staff to keep up with increased demands without sufficient staff is an exercise in futility. This is actually reflected in a Reddit post from a Starbucks barista just days ago, in which one barista described "upper management" getting increasingly "strict." While this might be brushed aside, the post also details being written up over progressively minor points. This represents a larger issue at work; if things that were not cause for being written up suddenly are, then it suggests that Starbucks is being increasingly exacting over its baristas' performance. Granted, this is anecdotal evidence only, but it does speak to a larger potential problem in place.

That sets up the rationale for the entire weak link: Starbucks wants more out of its baristas. It is actively punishing those who do not provide. Meanwhile, there are few reports to suggest that Starbucks is offering anything more to those baristas to carry out all these new functions. Demanding more for less is not a good way to encourage staff, and also offers a partial explanation of why the union movement seems to be carrying on, despite Starbucks' almost desperate insistence that it offers one of the best jobs in retail.

Less Stick, More Carrot

Which brings us to the inevitable flip side here. If Starbucks is putting this much weight on the baristas' backs, then what can it do differently to make the Back to Starbucks plan worthwhile? The simplest, most obvious moves apply here first, of course. Starbucks likes to point out its benefits. Starbucks particularly points out its "18 weeks of parental leave," which is great, if you happen to be a recent parent. For literally anyone else—like the legions of teenagers that Starbucks employs—it falls a bit short. It's like offering unlimited horn polishing services to, say, a bear.

Meanwhile, Starbucks' idea to make the baristas the front line in welcoming customers is perfectly rational. The problem, of course, is that they need to have time to do it. If they are making drinks and filling orders, they cannot be expected to also be kind and welcoming.

Further, as we have seen in recent days, the plan to make Starbucks warm and welcoming depends on Starbucks' baristas including cup art and thoughtful, personalized messages. Those take time to generate. And with reports suggesting that Starbucks brass is actually penalizing some reps for "insufficient sincerity," it is a recipe for disaster. Or even further unionization.

We have also seen the darker side of messages and cup art; the recent issue of a sheriff's deputy receiving what looked like a pig drawn on a cup was at best an unfortunate accident, at worst, a direct insult. Never mind that the pig in question might actually have been a bear, or even a slightly misshapen frog. Expecting baristas to generate professional-quality art on the side of a to-go cup in a matter of seconds is ludicrous.

Thus, the most simple fix here is, simply, to choose one. Either get your employees time to do the job right, or downscale the job to fit the time available. Don't expect professional artists to work for barista wages, and generate unique, warm sentiments in seconds. Consider printing your own messages on cups in advance; Culver's Frozen Custard does this with cups and sandwich containers alike. Consider also reopening the mobile-only pickup locations to take the weight off of location employees; those mobile-only platforms were great for people who just wanted to grab a drink and go.

There are plenty of options here. But with Back to Starbucks already well underway and running into issues, it is perhaps time to reconsider either the scope of the project or the quality of the inputs therein.

Is Starbucks Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on SBUX stock based on 15 Buys, eight Holds, and two Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 16.67% loss in its share price over the past year, the average SBUX price target of $100.59 per share implies 7.25% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Alphabet or Palantir: Cathie Wood Loads Up on One High-Conviction AI Stock
data.description:
AI-themed stocks have been all the rage on Wall Street for the past few years, but at the start of 2026, the landscape appears to be shifting. Worries about the amount being spent on the buildout of AI infrastructure, along with concerns about valuations, have led to a more cautious environment. That has been evident in a volatile market defined by constant swings of sentiment.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Volatility is not something Cathie Wood necessarily shies away from, even stating that "volatility is not a bad word," and seeing it as an unavoidable feature of investing in disruptive innovation.

You could say both Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOGL) and Palantir (NASDAQ:PLTR) operate at the forefront of AI-led innovation and both have found a place in Wood's Ark Invest portfolio. But recently, Wood has been loading up on one of these names while reducing her exposure to the other.

So, let's find out which high-conviction AI stock the Ark CEO currently favors, and with some assistance from the TipRanks database, we can see whether the Street's analysts see her choices in a positive light.

Alphabet

Alphabet has been investing in AI for many years, long before the current generative AI boom pushed the technology into the mainstream. From foundational research at DeepMind to in-house development of large language models and custom silicon like TPUs, the company has steadily built a vertically integrated AI stack.

But, interestingly, when genAI started to attract mainstream attention, Alphabet was seen as not quite as well-positioned as peers like Microsoft, an early backer of ChatGPT maker OpenAI. The talk was of disruption risk, with concerns that offerings like ChatGPT could erode Google's online search dominance.

But sentiment did eventually turn in Alphabet's favor as the company started showing its AI game was very strong. The launch of Gemini 3 helped cement that shift, with the model outperforming rivals such as ChatGPT on several key benchmarks. Just as importantly, people are actually using it. Gemini apps now reach 750 million monthly active users, and uptake among enterprise customers has been particularly strong.

The shift in narrative made Alphabet the Mag 7 stock to own last year, but the gains have been harder to come by in 2026 as general sentiment toward AI high-flyers has become more cautious, particularly as the hyperscalers – Alphabet among them – intend to ramp up AI-related spend considerably this year.

But Cathie Wood seems undaunted by all that and must like the opportunity offered here. During 4Q25, ARK Invest bought 206,936 shares, upping its GOOGL stake by 62%.

For J.P. Morgan's Doug Anmuth, all the money being spent by Alphabet on the AI buildout will be worth it.

"We believe the doubling of capex comes from a position of strength, but there is significant focus on depreciation, which will accelerate in 2026, & FCF, which we model to fall to $29B (-61% Y/Y)," Anmuth, who ranks among the top 4% of Street stock experts, said. "However, we believe the valuation framework for many remains anchored around 25x-30x on $14+ in 2027 GAAP EPS. Google, Meta, and Amazon are all meaningfully pressuring their cash generation. However, we believe GOOGL has a broad set of monetization levers, including Search, Cloud, and a growing AIdriven ad opportunity, with the massive growth in backlog providing a tangible bridge to future revenue and margin expansion."

"Additionally," the 5-star analyst added, "we believe GOOGL is best positioned to support the large jump in capex as GOOGL currently has $80B in net cash, ahead of AMZN's $57B & META's & $23B, and should generate the most cash in 2026. Google's full stack AI differentiation, as highlighted in the 10-K, further reinforces our conviction in the company's ability to drive multi-year value from these investments… GOOGL remains our top pick coming out of Internet Mag 7 earnings."

Accordingly, Anmuth rates the shares as Overweight (i.e., Buy), while his $395 price target points toward 12-month returns of 29%. (To watch Anmuth's track record, click here)

Elsewhere on the Street, the stock claims an additional 26 Buys and 7 Holds, for a Strong Buy consensus rating. Going by the $377.23 average price target, a year from now, shares will be changing hands for a 22% premium. (See GOOGL stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: Goldman Sachs (GS) Key Insider Sells $28M in Stock. Should Investors Worry?
data.description:
Goldman Sachs (GS) saw a notable wave of insider selling over the past week, with several top executives selling shares worth more than $28 million. While insider sales do not always signal trouble, they often catch investors' attention, especially when several executives sell shares at the same time.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The largest transaction came from John F.W. Rogers, Goldman's longtime executive vice president. On February 11, 2026, Rogers sold 15,855 shares of the company, valued at about $15.21 million.

Two days earlier, on February 9, Chief Financial Officer Denis P. Coleman executed an informative sale of 11,623 shares, totaling about $10.94 million. Also, Chief Risk Officer Alex S. Golten sold 2,292 shares, worth nearly $2.14 million.

Insider Sentiment for Goldman Sachs Stock Is Bearish

It is worth noting that Goldman Sachs has witnessed a significant wave of insider selling in early 2026, with top executives divesting over $112 million in stock over the last 90 days. These transactions come as the stock trades near all-time highs following strong Q4 earnings and a 12.5% dividend hike.

Overall, TipRanks' Insider Trading Activity Tool shows that insider confidence in GS stock is currently Very Negative.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: When Is the Hedge Fund 13F Deadline?
data.description:
Every quarter, hedge funds and other institutional investors are required to submit a 13F filing to the SEC, documenting their stock and options positions for the previous quarter. The deadline for the fourth quarter falls on Tuesday, February 17.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The deadline is usually 45 days after the end of a quarter, which falls on February 14 for the quarter ended December 31. However, February 14 occurred on a Saturday, while the following Monday landed on Presidents Day.

Renaissance, Baillie Gifford, and Baupost File Ahead of Schedule

Ahead of the deadline, several hedge funds have already filed their 13Fs. Here's a look at what Renaissance Technologies, Baillie Gifford, and Baupost Group did during the fourth quarter.

Renaissance Technologies

  • Top Five Positions: Palantir (PLTR), United Therapeutics (UTHR), Micron (MU), Kinross Gold (KGC), VeriSign (VRSN)
  • Top Buys: Costco (COST), Micron (MU), Tesla (TSLA), Netflix (NFLX), Procter & Gamble (PG)
  • Top Sales: Nvidia (NVDA), Alphabet (GOOGL), Alphabet (GOOG), AppLovin (APP), Gilead Sciences (GILD)

Baillie Gifford

  • Top Five Positions: Nvidia, MercadoLibre (MELI), Amazon (AMZN), Shopify (SHOP), Sea Limited (SE)
  • Top Buys: Axon (AXON), Medline (MDLN), Rocket Lab (RKLB), Alphabet, Duolingo (DUOL)
  • Top Sales: Datadog (DDOG), Cloudflare (NET), Shopify, BioNTech (BNTX), Meta Platforms (META)

Baupost Group

  • Top Five Positions: Restaurant Brands International (QSR), Amazon, Willis Towers Watson (WTW), Elevance Health (ELV), Union Pacific (UNP)
  • Top Buys: Amazon, Molina Healthcare (MOH), Group Aeromexico (AERO), Willis Towers Watson, Fiserv (FISV)
  • Top Sales: CRH (CRH), Alphabet, Dollar General (DG), Liberty Global Class A (LBTYA), PagSeguro (PAGS)

data.title: Tesla Adds Grok to Its Vehicles in Nine European Countries
data.description:
EV maker Tesla (TSLA) is bringing xAI's Grok to its vehicles across Europe, starting with nine countries. According to Tesla's Europe, Middle East & Africa account on X, the first phase includes the United Kingdom, Ireland, Germany, Switzerland, Austria, Italy, France, Portugal, and Spain, with more markets expected to be added later. Interestingly, Grok will allow drivers to ask questions using real-time information and interact with the system without taking their hands off the wheel.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In addition, the feature includes selectable personalities, such as "Storyteller" and "Unhinged," that will give drivers different response styles. To activate Grok, users can either open it through the App Launcher or press and hold the microphone button on the steering wheel. However, Grok is only available on Model S, Model 3, Model X, Model Y, and Cybertruck vehicles that have an AMD (AMD) infotainment processor.

Vehicles must also be running software version 2025.26 or newer, and navigation command support requires version 2025.44.25 or later. Moreover, Tesla noted that Grok does not replace standard voice commands for vehicle controls like climate settings or media changes. The company also said that Grok interactions are processed securely by xAI and are not connected to individual drivers or specific vehicles. For now, users do not need a separate Grok account or a subscription to access the feature.

What Is the Prediction for TSLA Stock?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average TSLA price target of $396.80 per share implies 5% downside risk.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: U.S. Dollar Sentiment at Weakest Level Since 2012, Shows BofA Survey
data.description:
The U.S. Dollar Index, which measures the dollar's value against six major currencies, has fallen by 1.2% year-to-date. More downside could be on the way, according to a Bank of America survey of fund managers.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The survey showed that dollar positioning was the most negative since 2012, the year when the bank started to collect data for the statistic.

Weaker Dollar Could Benefit U.S. Growth and Corporate Profits

The falling sentiment comes alongside concerns over the Fed's independence and heightened geopolitical tensions, which could shake confidence in the U.S. economy and reduce foreign demand for the dollar.

"We still see an environment where the dollar can weaken from here," said JPMorgan Asset Management's Iain Stealey.

At the same time, a weaker dollar has benefits as well, such as boosting U.S. exports, increasing earnings for multinational businesses, and lifting gross domestic product (GDP) growth.

Never Ending Story

data.title: IBM Plans to Triple Entry-Level Hiring, Even for AI-Exposed Roles
data.description:
Tech giant IBM (IBM) is taking a different approach to artificial intelligence than many of its peers. Instead of reducing hiring for roles that AI is expected to automate, the company plans to triple entry-level hiring in the U.S. in 2026. Speaking at the Leading with AI Summit in New York, Chief Human Resources Officer Nickle LaMoreaux said that this increase will apply even to jobs that many believe AI can now handle. In other words, IBM does not see automation as a reason to shrink its junior-level workforce, as it believes that human employees still play an essential role alongside AI tools.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At the same time, IBM has restructured those entry-level roles. Because AI can now complete many routine coding tasks, junior software developers are spending less time writing basic code and more time working directly with customers. Similarly, entry-level HR employees step in when AI chatbots are unable to fully resolve employee questions by helping correct outputs and communicating with managers.

Importantly, LaMoreaux explained that companies that stop hiring early-career workers are at risk of creating a long-term talent gap, as they will not have enough experienced employees in the future. As a result, IBM is focusing on redesigning roles rather than eliminating them, at a time when many tech leaders are warning that AI could significantly reduce entry-level opportunities in the coming years. However, LaMoreaux pushed back on that view by arguing that IBM's strategy is to combine human skills with AI capabilities rather than replace workers entirely.

Is IBM a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on IBM stock based on 13 Buys, four Holds, and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average IBM price target of $337.53 per share implies 28.6% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Nuclear Stocks Ready to Glow as Small Reactor Takes Cargo Ride Over California
data.description:
Nuclear stocks could be all a glow tomorrow when U.S. markets reopen after a small reactor took a ride on a cargo plane over California at the weekend.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Quick Deployment Power

The U.S. Department of Energy and Defense said that a small nuclear reactor had been transported for the first time ever from California to Utah, demonstrating the mobility of the technology and the potential to quickly deploy power for military and civilian use.

The agencies partnered with California-based Valar Atomics to fly one of the company's Ward microreactors on a C-17 aircraft — without nuclear fuel — to Hill Air Force Base in Utah. Energy Secretary Chris Wright and Under Secretary of Defense for Acquisition and Sustainment Michael Duffey were on the C-17 flight with the reactor and its components.

"This gets us closer to deploy nuclear power when and where it is needed to give our nation's warfighters the tools to win in battle," Duffey said.

It's not just for use in conflict. President Trump sees small nuclear reactors as being a crucial way of boosting domestic U.S. energy production, rather than his hated 'windmills' and renewable energy.

Trump last May issued four executive orders aimed at boosting domestic nuclear deployment to meet growing demand for energy for national security and the growth of energy hungry AI data centers.

In December, the Energy Department issued two new grants to speed up development.

Much Needed Boost

The Energy Department plans to have three microreactors reach "criticality" — when a nuclear reaction can sustain itself — by July 4.

The microreactor on the plane, just bigger than a minivan, is capable of generating up to 5 megawatts of electricity, enough to power 5,000 homes. It will start operating in July at 100 kilowatts and peak at 250 kilowatts this year before ramping up to full capacity.

Valar hopes to start selling power on a test basis in 2027 and become fully commercial in 2028.

The cargo ride could give a lift to NuScale Power (SMR), whose stock price dropped around 21% in 2025 given concerns over commercial agreements.

Nano Nuclear Energy (NNE), which is developing ZEUS, a solid-core battery reactor, and ODIN, a low-pressure coolant reactor, is down 21.30% over the last 12 months. It has been hit by mounting operating losses and a critical bottleneck in the nuclear fuel supply chain that could influence the pace of future deployments.
data.author: David Craik
